status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

current_baseline.scope: Repository-process validation plus .venv dependency and test execution whenever Python is available.
current_baseline.last_result: 2026-06-27: after merging the root agent layer, init.ps1 passed outside the sandbox using D:\TestFlow\.venv and the full test suite passed with 8 tests. Demo command `python main.py --target examples/calculator.py` exited 0, wrote generated_tests/test_calculator.py, and reported 100% pass rate / 100% target-file coverage. A package shim `testflow/llm_client.py` was added so merged agents can import `testflow.llm_client`. `.env` placeholders, lightweight env loading, optional Langfuse tracing, and `scripts/run_agent_smoke.py` were added for direct agent testing. Inside the default sandbox, .venv Python cannot access its base interpreter under C:\Users. Bash remains blocked because WSL has no installed distro.

features.title: Adapt long-running agent process docs to TestFlow
features.user_visible_behavior: A new agent can read the repository artifacts, understand that TestFlow is an execution-guided unit test orchestrator, run the current baseline, and identify the next product feature without guessing.
verification: Run the PowerShell baseline command from the repository root.
Verify feature_list.json is valid JSON and names TestFlow features rather than template chat features.
Verify progress, handoff, quality, checklist, evaluator, AGENTS, and CLAUDE docs describe the current TestFlow project.
Verify canonical filenames without the imported ' (1)' suffix exist and duplicate suffixed files are removed.
evidence: 2026-06-27: powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ./init.ps1 exited 0 and reported docs-only baseline passed.
2026-06-27: feature_list.json parses as JSON via init.ps1 ConvertFrom-Json.
2026-06-27: canonical process artifact filenames were added and imported duplicate suffixed copies were removed.
features.notes: This documentation adaptation task is complete. Canonical filenames are the source of truth; imported duplicate files with the ' (1)' suffix were removed.

features.title: Create installable Python CLI skeleton
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can install the package locally and run testflow --help plus a no-op analysis command without errors.
verification: Add pyproject.toml with console script entry point testflow.
Add src/testflow package with CLI module.
Run the standard baseline and verify package installation uses .venv.
Run .venv TestFlow executable --help.
Run .venv Python -m pytest tests/.
features.notes: Partially implemented by main.py demo CLI. Still not passing for this feature because there is no pyproject.toml or installable console script named testflow yet.

features.title: Analyze Python target project with AST
features.user_visible_behavior: A developer can run analysis on a small Python project and receive a structured summary of public functions, classes, exceptions, imports, and testability risks.
verification: Create a small example Python project.
Run testflow analyze ./examples/calculator.
Verify output includes discovered functions/classes and exception paths.
Run unit tests for the analyzer.
features.notes: A root-level AnalyzerAgent was merged and now imports successfully in tests, but this feature is not passing until it is wired into an installable CLI/analyze command and verified end to end.

features.title: Introduce runtime state and heuristic planner
features.user_visible_behavior: A TestFlow run records execution state and chooses the next agent based on pass rate, failure type, coverage, and quality signals.
verification: Add state model covering project, analysis, test inventory, execution, coverage, quality, and budget.
Add heuristic planner matching README rules.
Run unit tests for planner decisions across syntax error, failing tests, low coverage, weak assertions, and stop state.

features.title: Run pytest and capture execution feedback
features.user_visible_behavior: TestFlow can execute pytest for a target project and persist exit code, pass/fail counts, tracebacks, syntax errors, and import errors.
verification: Run against an example project with passing tests.
Run against an example project with failing tests.
Verify parsed execution state matches pytest output.
Run unit tests for pytest output parsing.

features.title: Generate initial pytest files from analysis and edge cases
features.user_visible_behavior: TestFlow writes generated pytest files into a tests directory without modifying production code.
verification: Run generation on examples/calculator.
Verify generated tests are written to tests/test_*_generated.py.
Verify production source files are unchanged.
Run generated tests and record result.
evidence: 2026-06-27: `D:\TestFlow\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\run_agent_smoke.py --target examples\calculator.py` exited 0, discovered 5 functions, and wrote `generated_tests\test_agent_smoke.py`.

2026-06-27: `D:\TestFlow\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\run_real_flow.py --target examples\string_utils.py` exited 0, generated `generated_tests\test_string_utils_real.py`, ran 12 generated tests, reported 100% coverage, wrote coverage XML/HTML artifacts under `.testflow`, and printed a Langfuse trace URL.

2026-06-27: Langfuse API check for real flow trace `037b6b3c7eb766624afdb1c40fd83e17` found trace name `testflow-real-flow` with 9 observations across CHAIN, AGENT, GENERATION, TOOL, and EVALUATOR types; the LLM generation observation is nested under the generate-tests agent observation.

2026-06-27: `D:\TestFlow\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\run_real_flow.py --target examples\string_utils.py` now calls all six agents. Langfuse API check for trace `e0b4d251ab865c6b2c0964bcaa3bdff5` found 14 observations with types `AGENT: 6`, `GENERATION: 3`, `TOOL: 3`, `EVALUATOR: 1`, and `CHAIN: 1`.
features.notes: A root-level generator/repair/coverage/verifier agent layer was merged and import-smoked. `scripts/run_agent_smoke.py` can exercise Analyzer, EdgeCase, and TestGenerator directly; with OPENAI_API_KEY in .env it calls the real model and traces via Langfuse if LANGFUSE keys are configured. `scripts/run_real_flow.py` is the easier real-agent command: it reads targets from `test_data/` by default, writes generated pytest files to `generated_tests/`, writes coverage XML/HTML reports under `.testflow/`, and prints the current Langfuse trace URL. Filter Langfuse by trace name `testflow-real-flow` to see the full six-agent flow; LLM generation observations are nested under generator, repair, and coverage agents. Runtime still uses deterministic orchestrator fallbacks for the main demo path until agent integration is explicitly completed.

features.title: Repair generated tests from execution failures
features.user_visible_behavior: When generated tests fail due to syntax, import, fixture, async marker, or oracle mismatch in generated test code, TestFlow repairs the tests and reruns them.
verification: Use fixtures with known import and assertion failures.
Run TestFlow and verify generated tests are patched.
Verify production files are not modified.
Verify rerun result is recorded.

features.title: Map coverage gaps to targeted test suggestions
features.user_visible_behavior: TestFlow reads coverage output and identifies missing lines and likely behavior to target next.
verification: Run pytest with coverage on an example project.
Parse coverage.xml.
Verify missing lines are mapped to source files and suggested tests.
Run unit tests for coverage parsing.

features.title: Verify assertion quality and export final report
features.user_visible_behavior: TestFlow flags weak or duplicated tests and writes a final report with pass rate, coverage, generated files, repairs, and remaining gaps.
verification: Run verifier against fixtures with weak assertions and duplicates.
Run a full example flow that emits reports/testflow_report.md.
Verify report includes planner trace and remaining gaps.
Run unit tests for report rendering.
